seo.title: Building Agentic RAG with Google ADK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build agentic rag with Google ADK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Agentic RAG with Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) enables multi-agent systems that autonomously plan retrieval queries, fetch from diverse sources, and synthesize attributed responses using Gemini models. However, multi-agent delegation, model switching, and long-session state management introduce critical failure modes like circular dependencies and hallucinated citations. Vex guardrails provide runtime verification for source faithfulness, query accuracy, and consistency, ensuring reliable production deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: Agentic RAG Architecture with Google ADK
architectureOverview.description:
The Agentic RAG architecture in Google ADK features a Planner Agent that decomposes user queries into sub-queries, delegates to specialized Retriever Agents for vector stores and knowledge bases, and routes to a Synthesizer Agent for response generation with citations. Each agent maintains session state across Gemini model interactions, with tools for query execution and chunk retrieval. The workflow handles multi-hop reasoning by iteratively refining retrieval plans based on intermediate results.

Retriever Agents interface with multiple sources (Pinecone, enterprise search, web APIs) while the Synthesizer enforces attribution formatting. Session state tracks query lineage and source metadata to prevent duplication. Gemini model switching between agents requires consistent output schemas to avoid delegation failures.

This modular design excels at complex knowledge tasks but risks circular delegation loops, state corruption in long conversations, and format mismatches between tool responses.
architectureOverview.diagram:
User Query
    ↓
[Planner Agent] → decompose → [sub-queries]
    ↓
[Retriever Agent 1] → Pinecone/VectorDB
    ↓
[Retriever Agent 2] → Enterprise Search
    ↓
[Synthesizer Agent] → attribution + response
    ↓
Final Answer w/ Citations

risks.name: Circular Delegation Loops
risks.description: Planner Agent delegates to Retriever which re-delegates back to Planner, creating infinite loops
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies delegation DAG structure and terminates cycles

risks.name: Hallucinated Source Attribution
risks.description: Synthesizer Agent fabricates citations not present in retrieved chunks
risks.mitigation: Vex performs source faithfulness checks against retrieved context

risks.name: Query Planning Drift
risks.description: Planner generates irrelevant sub-queries diverging from original intent
risks.mitigation: Vex validates query alignment with semantic similarity thresholds

risks.name: Cross-Source Inconsistency
risks.description: Different retrievers return conflicting facts about same entity
risks.mitigation: Vex detects and flags entity conflicts across sources

implementation.title: Planner Agent with Query Decomposition
implementation.description: Core Planner Agent that decomposes complex queries into retrievable sub-queries
implementation.code:
from google_adk import Agent, Tool
from vex import Vex

planner_agent = Agent(
    name="planner",
    model="gemini-2.0-flash",
    instruction="Decompose complex queries into 3-5 atomic retrieval sub-queries."
)

@planner_agent.tool
def decompose_query(query: str) -> list[str]:
    """Return list of atomic sub-queries."""
    return [
        f"Find definitions for {query}",
        f"Recent updates about {query}",
        f"Key examples of {query}"
    ]

planner_agent.compile()

vexIntegration.title: Vex Before-Agent Callback Setup
vexIntegration.description: Install Vex callback to verify inputs before every agent execution
vexIntegration.code:
import vex
from google_adk import Callback

# Initialize Vex client
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="vex_your_key")

@Callback.before_agent
def vex_guardrail(ctx):
    """Vex verification before agent execution."""
    agent_name = ctx.agent.name
    input_data = ctx.input
    session_id = ctx.session_id
    
    # High-risk verifications for Agentic RAG
    results = vex_client.verify(
        session_id=session_id,
        verifications=[
            "source_faithfulness",
            "hallucination_detection",
            "query_planning_accuracy",
            "cross_source_consistency",
            "citation_verification"
        ],
        context={
            "agent": agent_name,
            "input": input_data,
            "retrieval_sources": ctx.get("sources", [])
        },
        risk_profile="high"
    )
    
    if not all(r.pass for r in results):
        raise Exception(f"Vex blocked: {results}")
    
# Register globally
workflow.add_callback(vex_guardrail)

vexIntegration.title: Source Faithfulness Verification
vexIntegration.description: Custom Vex verification ensuring synthesis stays faithful to retrieved sources
vexIntegration.code:
def source_faithfulness_verification(ctx):
    """Vex custom verification for RAG faithfulness."""
    generated_text = ctx.input.get("synthesis")
    source_chunks = ctx.context.get("retrieval_sources", [])
    
    result = vex_client.verify_single(
        name="source_faithfulness",
        input=generated_text,
        reference_documents=[c["content"] for c in source_chunks],
        threshold=0.85  # 85% chunk coverage required
    )
    
    return {
        "pass": result["faithfulness_score"] > 0.85,
        "score": result["faithfulness_score"],
        "missing_coverage": result["unsupported_spans"]
    }

# Register as custom verification
vex_client.register_verification(source_faithfulness_verification)
